rulings.Case Title: STEVEN MANN ET AL VS. AJLOUN ENTERPRISES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 31, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT AJLOUN ENTERPRISES LLC, MUATH ZGHOUL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs GRANTED. No opposition filed. The Court is in receipt of an Amended Memorandum of Costs. (A courtesy copy of this Amended Memorandum was accompanied by a letter, which does not appear to have been cc'd to all parties. The letter is not considered by the Court). The Amended Memorandum of Costs on file is not supported by any authority allowing the filing of an Amended Memorandum of Costs 1.5 months after the filing of the initial Memorandum of Costs. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RAMESH SHRESTHA VS. HOTEL SUNRISE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 31, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT HOTEL SUNRISE, INC., KIRITKUMAR PATEL, SHAKUNTLA PATEL Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees OFF CALENDAR, per request of the moving party. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARTHA RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 31, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Nothing on the face of the complaint or judicially noticeable documents indicate that co-owners of the property are necessary (or indispensable) parties to this action. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the propositions that non-signatory co-owners are entitled to enforce the contract seeking unpaid rent as a matter of law under all factual circumstances. Defendant further fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff has to include the allegations regarding potentially necessary parties in the complaint. Whether the co-owners are necessary parties is an issue of fact that cannot be determined at this stage of the litigation. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LIZA M DIGDIGAN VS. BANK OF AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-18-661838 WITH CASE CGC-18-569224
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF LIZA DIGDIGAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-18-661838 WITH CASE CGC-18-569224 GRANTED as follows: Unlawful detainer action is consolidated with the Civil Action for purposes of trial and discovery. Unlawful detainer action to proceed on the general civil action timeline. Hearing Required to address payment of taxes, insurance, and maintenance of the property for the duration of the litigation. Counsel to meet and confer prior to the hearing to address these issues and provide the Court with the specific numbers (e.g. taxes and insurance paid by Digdigan when she was the title owner; taxes and insurance paid now, etc.) and a working plan to proceed while Freemont holds legal title and Digdigan is in possession. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: EDELIZA ANUNCIACION VS. LORNA ABAAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT LORNA ABAAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Triable issue of fact re: whether Heather Abaan is an "Eligible Tenant." The Court notes that Defendant failed to properly introduce evidence (no objection by Plaintiff) and appears to have failed to properly edit the Separate Statement (e.g. facts 1, 10, 11; Compare with Separate Statement in action CUD-18-662435 filed on August 22, 2018). = (501/CFH)
